title: Helen of Troy
summary: Helen, also known as Helen of Troy, or Helen of Sparta, and in Latin as Helena, was a figure in Greek mythology said to have been the most beautiful woman in the world. She was believed to have been the daughter of Zeus by Leda or Nemesis, and the sister of Clytemnestra, Castor, Pollux, Philonoe, Phoebe and Timandra. She was first married to King Menelaus of Sparta. Her subsequent marriage to (or abduction by) Paris, a prince of Troy, brought about the Trojan War. In one version of the myth, Zeus came to Leda in the form of a swan; from that union Helen was born.
